title: Year 8 New Zealand Curriculum - Ministry of Education
content:
## Year 8 New Zealand Curriculum
Information to help you understand what your child will have learned by their eighth year at school, and guidance on ways you can help them further their learning at home.
- Guide for the New Zealand Curriculum – Years 0 to 8
- First 6 months at school
- Year 1 New Zealand Curriculum
- Year 2 New Zealand Curriculum
- Year 3 New Zealand Curriculum
- Year 4 New Zealand Curriculum
- Year 5 New Zealand Curriculum
- Year 6 New Zealand Curriculum
- Year 7 New Zealand Curriculum
- Year 8 New Zealand Curriculum Mathematics and Statistics in Year 8 English in Year 8 Science in Year 8 Health and Physical Education in Year 8 Technology in Year 8 Social Sciences in Year 8 The Arts in Year 8
- Mathematics and Statistics in Year 8
- Health and Physical Education in Year 8
- Technology in Year 8
- Social Sciences in Year 8
We’re inviting parents and caregivers to share their feedback on the Parent Portal to help us improve the information and support we provide. Take a few minutes to complete our short survey.
Parent Portal survey – Survey Monkey

## By the end of Year 8 #
Knowing what your child is learning will help you support and guide them as they begin learning reading, writing and maths every day.
- Mathematics and Statistics in Year 8 In Years 7 and 8, the focus is on helping children see themselves as part of a bigger world and encouraging them to develop greater independence. Find out more

### Mathematics and Statistics in Year 8
In Years 7 and 8, the focus is on helping children see themselves as part of a bigger world and encouraging them to develop greater independence.
- English in Year 8 For English in Year 8, your child’s teacher will focus on oral language, reading, and writing. Find out more
For English in Year 8, your child’s teacher will focus on oral language, reading, and writing.
- Science in Year 8 In Year 8, your child will compare and interpret data and apply logical reasoning to explain their findings. Find out more
In Year 8, your child will compare and interpret data and apply logical reasoning to explain their findings.
- Health and Physical Education in Year 8 In Year 8, your child will learn about managing puberty, safe relationships, sex education and sexual health, sports techniques and survival skills. Find out more

### Health and Physical Education in Year 8
In Year 8, your child will learn about managing puberty, safe relationships, sex education and sexual health, sports techniques and survival skills.
- Technology in Year 8 In Year 8, your child works on more complex projects that follow a rigorous design process, exploring how all parts of a system interact and combining physical and digital tools for reliable outcomes. Find out more

### Technology in Year 8
In Year 8, your child works on more complex projects that follow a rigorous design process, exploring how all parts of a system interact and combining physical and digital tools for reliable outcomes.
- Social Sciences in Year 8 In Year 8, your child will start to connect important concepts such as governance, rights, and global connection, learning about government structures, personal finance and the economy. Find out more

### Social Sciences in Year 8
In Year 8, your child will start to connect important concepts such as governance, rights, and global connection, learning about government structures, personal finance and the economy.
- The Arts in Year 8 In Year 8, your child will engage with diverse art forms and traditions as well as deepening their artistic skills through practice and reflection. Find out more
In Year 8, your child will engage with diverse art forms and traditions as well as deepening their artistic skills through practice and reflection.
